title: ceratostylis spathulata
intro: Ceratostylis spathulata, a type of orchid native to tropical Asia, is a terrestrial orchid known for its unique spathe-like leaves and white, waxy flowers. Traditionally, the plant has been used in herbal medicine, particularly in Southeast Asia, where it is valued for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits of Ceratostylis spathulata are said to include relief from fever, coughs, and respiratory issues, as well as its potential to lower blood sugar levels and improve wound healing. In terms of herbal preparations, Ceratostylis spathulata is often used in traditional medicine as a decoction, made by boiling the roots and leaves in water, or as a powder, taken orally to harness its medicinal properties.
